Japanese: ９月７日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第１法廷
English: September 7, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 1

Japanese: これより、綾里　真宵の 法廷を開廷します。
English: The court is now in session for the trial of Ms. Maya Fey.

Japanese: 検察側、 準備完了しております。
English: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 弁護側、 準備完了しております。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: （御剣　怜侍‥‥か）
English: (Miles Edgeworth...)

Japanese: （今日は、どんな小さなスキも 見せられないな‥‥）
English: (I'd better not show any signs of weakness today, or he'll be on me in an instant.)

Japanese: 御剣検事。 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Edgeworth. Please give the court your opening statement.

Japanese: 被告人・綾里　真宵は、 事件当時、殺人現場にいた。
English: Thank you, Your Honor. The defendant, Ms. Maya Fey, was at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: 検察側は、彼女が犯行を 行ったことを示す証拠と、
English: The prosecution has evidence she committed this murder...

Japanese: 犯行を目撃したという証人を 用意している。
English: and we have a witness who saw her do it.

Japanese: 被告人の有罪は、１点の うたがう余地もないだろう。
English: The prosecution sees no reason to doubt the facts of this case, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。
English: I see.

Japanese: では、さっそく 始めてもらいましょう。
English: Thank you, Mr. Edgeworth. Let's begin then.

Japanese: 最初の証人を入廷させて いただこう。
English: You may call your first witness.

Japanese: 捜査の指揮をとった、 イトノコギリ刑事！
English: The prosecution calls the chief officer at the scene, Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: 証人。名前と職業は？
English: Witness, please state your name and profession to the court.

Japanese: はッ！
English: Sir!

Japanese: 自分の名前は、糸鋸　圭介 （いとのこぎりけいすけ）ッス！
English: My name's Dick Gumshoe, sir!

Japanese: 所轄署の、殺人事件捜査担当の 刑事ッス！
English: I'm the detective in charge of homicides down at the precinct, sir!

Japanese: イトノコギリ刑事。
English: Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: まず、事件の説明を おねがいする。
English: Please, describe for us the details of this murder.

Japanese: はッ！ では、上面図で説明するッス。
English: Very well, sir! Let me use this floor map of the office to explain.

Japanese: 死体は、窓際のこの位置に 倒れていたッス。
English: The body was found by this window, here.

Japanese: 被害者の死因は？
English: And the cause of death?

Japanese: 鈍器で殴られた際の、失血ッス。
English: Loss of blood due to being struck by a blunt object, sir!

Japanese: 凶器は《考える人》の置物で、 死体のそばに転がっていたッス！
English: The murder weapon was a statue of "The Thinker" found next to the body, sir!

Japanese: 女の子でも、じゅうぶん犯行が 可能な重さッス！
English: It was heavy enough to be a deadly weapon, even in a girl's hands, sir!

Japanese: では、その凶器の置物を 受理しましょう。
English: The court accepts the statue as evidence.

Japanese: （あいかわらず、“置物” あつかいか‥‥）
English: (They're still calling it a "statue"...)

Japanese: 証拠品《上面図》を 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Floor plans added to the Court Record.

Japanese: さて‥‥刑事。
English: Now, Detective...

Japanese: は、はい‥‥ッス。
English: Y-yes sir!

Japanese: キミは、現場にいた 綾里　真宵を、すぐ逮捕した。
English: You immediately arrested Ms. Maya Fey, who was found at the scene, correct?

Japanese: それは、なぜか？
English: Can you tell me why?

Japanese: 動かぬ証拠が あったからッス！
English: Yes sir! I had hard evidence she did it, sir!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ では、イトノコギリ刑事。
English: Hmm. Detective Gumshoe,

Japanese: その“動かぬ証拠”とやらを、 《証言》してください。
English: Please testify to the court about this "hard evidence."

Japanese: ～綾里　真宵を逮捕した理由～
English: -- Maya Fey's Arrest --

Japanese: 自分は、電話の通報で事件を知り、 現場へかけつけたッス！
English: As soon as the phone call came in, I rushed to the scene!

Japanese: 現場には、 ２人の人物がいたッス。
English: There were two people there already:

Japanese: 被告人の綾里　真宵と、 弁護人、成歩堂　龍一。
English: The defendant, Ms. Maya Fey, and the lawyer, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 自分は迷わず、 綾里　真宵を逮捕したッス！
English: I immediately arrested Ms. Maya Fey!

Japanese: その理由は！　通報者の 目撃証言があったからッス！
English: Why? We had a witness account describing her!

Japanese: その通報者は、綾里　真宵の 犯行の瞬間を見ていたッス！
English: The witness saw Ms. Maya Fey at the very moment of the murder!

Japanese: ふむう、 犯行の瞬間を見ていた‥‥。
English: Hmm... The very moment, you say.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。では成歩堂くん、 《尋問》をしてもらいましょう。
English: Very well. Mr. Wright, you may begin your cross-examination.

Japanese: は、はい。 （そんなこと言われても‥‥）
English: Y-yes, Your Honor.
(Cross-examine what...?)

Japanese: （今の証言に、ムジュンするような 部分なんて、あったか‥‥？）
English: (I couldn't see a single contradiction in that testimony...)
...whoosh...

Japanese: ‥‥コツン！
English: SMACK!

Japanese: （‥‥！　被告席の真宵ちゃんが、 何か投げてきたぞ）
English: (Hey! Maya just threw something at me...)

Japanese: （なになに）
English: (What's this?)

Japanese: （“お姉ちゃんは、証言に ムジュンが見つからないとき”）
English: ("When my sister couldn't find any contradictions in a witness's testimony")

Japanese: （“とりあえずハッタリをかけて、 証人をゆさぶっていました”）
English: ("she would bluff it and press the witness on every detail!")

Japanese: （“ゆさぶられた証人は‥‥”）
English: ("The witness always slips up and says something wrong...")

Japanese: （“動揺して 口をすべらせることが‥‥”）
English: ("It worked lots of times!")

Japanese: （‥‥ははあ、さすが オニ弁護士の妹、だな‥‥）
English: (Heh... I should have expected Maya would know some of her sister's tricks!)

Japanese: （よし。 やってみるか、ぼくも）
English: (Alright. Let's give this a try!)

Japanese: ‥‥どうかしましたか？
English: Something the matter?

Japanese: い、いえ。 では、《尋問》させてもらいます。
English: No, Your Honor. I'd like to begin my cross-examination.

Japanese: ～綾里　真宵を逮捕した理由～
English: -- Maya Fey's Arrest --

Japanese: 自分は、電話の通報で事件を知り、 現場へかけつけたッス！
English: As soon as the phone call came in, I rushed to the scene!

Japanese: 現場には、 ２人の人物がいたッス。
English: There were two people there already:

Japanese: 被告人の綾里　真宵と、 弁護人、成歩堂　龍一。
English: The defendant, Ms. Maya Fey, and the lawyer, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 自分は迷わず、 綾里　真宵を逮捕したッス！
English: I immediately arrested Ms. Maya Fey!

Japanese: その理由は！　通報者の 目撃証言があったからッス！
English: Why? We had a witness account describing her!

Japanese: その通報者は、綾里　真宵の 犯行の瞬間を見ていたッス！
English: The witness saw Ms. Maya Fey at the very moment of the murder!

Japanese: （この証言の内容じゃあ、 ムジュンしようがないよな）
English: (I can't see a single contradiction in there!)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんのヒントを信じて、 怪しい部分をゆさぶってみるか）
English: (I'll just have to take Maya's advice and press him on anything suspicious!)

Japanese: それは、誰からの 通報だったんですか？
English: Who did you say you got a call from?

Japanese: またまた！　知ってるくせに シラジラしいッス！
English: Hey pal, don't play dumb! You know who!

Japanese: 現場の向かい側にある、 板東ホテルの客ッス！
English: The call was from a customer at the Gatewater Hotel, right across from the crime scene!

Japanese: （‥‥ここをゆさぶっても イミ、ないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... okay, I pressed. Not sure it did much, though.)

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥それで？
English: Right. Please continue.

Japanese: 通報を受けてから現場に 到着するまで、
English: Detective Gumshoe, how long would you say it took,

Japanese: どれぐらいの時間が かかりましたか？
English: between you receiving the call, and your arrival at the scene of the crime?

Japanese: そうッスね‥‥たぶん、 ３分ぐらいだったッス！
English: Hmm, right... I'd say it was about 3 minutes!

Japanese: は、早いですね。
English: Th-that's pretty fast!

Japanese: ウチの署の今月の標語は、 “出前迅速”ッス！
English: Our motto this month is "quick response"!

Japanese: おかげで、ハンニンを 逃がさずにすんだッス！
English: That's how I got there before the killer got away!

Japanese: ‥‥それで？
English: Indeed!

Japanese: 現場にいた２人の人間とは、 誰だったか？
English: So, tell us who the two people you found on the scene were.

Japanese: はッ！
English: Yes sir!

Japanese: 間違いありませんか！
English: Are you absolutely sure it was us?

Japanese: スッとぼけるのも いいかげんにするッス！
English: Listen pal, your dumb act will only get you so far!

Japanese: 妙にマニアックな和服の少女に、 そのヘンなギザギザアタマ！
English: With her funky hippie clothes and your spiky hair?

Japanese: ２人とも、ちょっと 目立ちすぎッス！
English: You two stand out like... like suspicious people at a crime scene!

Japanese: （‥‥まあ、ぼくはともかく）
English: (Well... he does have a point about her.)

Japanese: （彼女を見間違えることは ないよな‥‥）
English: (She is pretty unmistakable.)

Japanese: （もっと、ゆさぶりをかける場所を よく考えた方がいいぞ‥‥）
English: (I should pick my points to press with a little more care...)

Japanese: なぜですか！ その理由をお聞きしたい！
English: Why's that? What's your reason?

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Hold on just one second!

Japanese: な、なんス？
English: Y-yeah?

Japanese: さっきあなたは、たしか こう言った。
English: If I heard correctly...

Japanese: “動かぬ証拠があったから” 被告人を逮捕した、と！
English: You said you arrested her because you had "hard evidence" she did it, correct?

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: そんなこと、言ったッスか？ ‥‥自分が？
English: Did... did I say that? Me?

Japanese: 言いましたよ！
English: I heard you say it!

Japanese: 言いましたね。
English: You did say it.

Japanese: 言った。
English: You said it.

Japanese: 怪しげなピンクの女性の証言の、 どこが《動かぬ証拠》なんですか！
English: Exactly what about this suspicious woman in pink's claim was "hard evidence"!?

Japanese: な、なんスとッ！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: 梅世さんは、怪しげでも ピンクでもないッス！
English: Miss May isn't suspicious, and she sure isn't pink, pal!

Japanese: い、いや‥‥ピンクは ピンクッスけど‥‥。
English: W-well, I guess she is pink...

Japanese: 決して、その。ワルいピンクではな
もうけっこうです。 ‥‥イトノコギリ刑事。
English: That's enough, Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: もっとハッキリした 証拠は、なかったんですか？
English: Do you have any more solid proof other than her claims, Detective?
Umm...

Japanese: （なるほど。“ゆさぶる” って、こういうことか‥‥）
English: (Hmm! I guess pressing can have its advantages!)

Japanese: あったッス。
English: Yes.
(Gah!)

Japanese: そッス。証言の順番を 間違えたッス！
English: Sorry, I got the order of things mixed up in my testimony, Your Honor Sir!

Japanese: こっちを先に言えばよかったッス！
English: There was something I should have told you about first, Your Honor!

Japanese: では、刑事。もう一度 《証言》してもらいましょう。
English: Very well, Detective. Let's hear your testimony again.

Japanese: ～動かぬ証拠～
English: -- Hard Evidence --

Japanese: 容疑者をカクホした自分は、 次に、この目で現場を調べたッス。
English: After securing the suspect, I examined the scene of the crime with my own eyes.

Japanese: そして死体のそばで、 メモを見つけたッス！
English: I found a memo written on a piece of paper next to the victim's body!

Japanese: あきらかに血で“マヨイ”と 書いてあったッス！
English: On it, the word "Maya" was written clearly in blood!

Japanese: 化学分析の結果、この血は 被害者のものに間違いないッス！
English: Lab test results showed that the blood was the victim's!

Japanese: そして、被害者の指には血が ついていたッス！
English: Also, there was blood found on the victim's finger!

Japanese: 被害者は死ぬ前に、 犯人の名前を書き残したッス！
English: Before she died, the victim wrote the killer's name!

Japanese: どうッス？ これが《動かぬ証拠》ッス。
English: How you like that? That's my "hard evidence"!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 《尋問》に入る前に、 刑事にヒトコト、言っておきます。
English: Before we begin cross- examination, I have a question for you, Detective.

Japanese: は、‥‥はッ？
English: Y-Your Honor?

Japanese: ‥‥なんで、先に コレを証言しなかったんですか！
English: Why didn't you testify about this vital piece of evidence the first time!?

Japanese: ‥‥いや。おはずかしい‥‥ッス。
English: Ah... eh... I know. I'm real embarrassed I forgot about it, Your Honor Sir.

Japanese: 気をつけなさい！
English: Try to be more careful!

Japanese: ‥‥では、弁護人。 《尋問》を。
English: Very well, the defense may begin its cross-examination.

Japanese: ～動かぬ証拠～
English: -- Hard Evidence --

Japanese: 容疑者をカクホした自分は、 次に、この目で現場を調べたッス。
English: After securing the suspect, I examined the scene of the crime with my own eyes.

Japanese: そして死体のそばで、 メモを見つけたッス！
English: I found a memo written on a piece of paper next to the victim's body!

Japanese: あきらかに血で“マヨイ”と 書いてあったッス！
English: On it, the word "Maya" was written clearly in blood!

Japanese: 化学分析の結果、この血は 被害者のものに間違いないッス！
English: Lab test results showed that the blood was the victim's!

Japanese: そして、被害者の指には血が ついていたッス！
English: Also, there was blood found on the victim's finger!

Japanese: 被害者は死ぬ前に、 犯人の名前を書き残したッス！
English: Before she died, the victim wrote the killer's name!

Japanese: （証言はここまでだな）
English: (That's his whole testimony...)

Japanese: （よし。ムジュンを たたきつけてやるとするか）
English: (Okay, there has to be a contradiction in there somewhere. Let's find it!)

Japanese: そのとき、何か証拠を 見つけたわけですか？
English: And did you find any evidence?

Japanese: まま。そうあわてちゃダメッス。
English: Now, now, don't jump the gun on me, pal.

Japanese: お楽しみはコレからッス。 ダマって聞いとくッス。
English: Just listen. I'm getting to the good part!

Japanese: （イヤな感じ‥‥）
English: (I got a bad feeling about this...)

Japanese: 死体のそばにあったからといって、 被害者が書いたとはかぎらない！
English: Just because you found it next to the body doesn't mean the victim wrote it!

Japanese: へええ。 じゃあ、誰が書いたッス？
English: Ho hoh! Then who did write it, smarty-pants?

Japanese: だ、誰がって‥‥
English: Wh-who? Um...

Japanese: は、犯人ですよ！ 決まってるでしょう！
English: Th-the killer! Anyone can see that!

Japanese: はん。
English: Hoh.

Japanese: 犯人が、自分の名前を書いたと 言うつもりッスか！
English: You're saying the killer wrote her OWN name!? Buddy, please!

Japanese: 彼女は、ハメられたんだ！
English: She was framed!

Japanese: ‥‥待ちたまえ。
English: Hold on.

Japanese: そういうことなら、 証拠を提出してもらおうか。
English: If that's the case, where's your evidence?
Ah... (Urk!)

Japanese: ハッ！　キミにはちょっと ムズカシイ注文だったかな！
English: Hah! I guess that was a bit of a tall order for you.

Japanese: ‥‥証拠なき者は、沈黙を 守ってもらいたいな。
English: Those without evidence shouldn't open their mouths, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: もらいたいッスな。
English: Yeah, pal!

Japanese: （くそお‥‥）
English: (Argh...!)

Japanese: たとえば、目撃証人の 梅世さんとか！
English: Well... it could have been the witness, Miss May!

Japanese: ‥‥待ちたまえ。
English: Hold on.

Japanese: 証人は、ホテルにいたのだ。
English: The witness was in her hotel room, not the office.

Japanese: バカも休みやすみ言いたまえ！
English: Try pulling the other leg, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 休みやすみにして もらいたいッスな！
English: Yeah, and while you're at it, pull mine too, pal!

Japanese: （くそお‥‥）
English: (Argh...!)

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくかもしれませんッ！
English: It... it could have been me!

Japanese: な、なんスと！ アンタだったッスか！
English: What! S-so it was you!

Japanese: い、いやいやいや。 たとえば、の話です！
English: No, no, no, I'm just saying it COULD have been me!

Japanese: でも、否定できますか？
English: Can you prove it wasn't?

Japanese: ‥‥待て。
English: Hold on.

Japanese: それなら、キミはそれを 認めるのか？
English: So, you admit to this?

Japanese: キミがあれを書いたのか？
English: You admit that you wrote the note?
Uh...

Japanese: いいか。
English: Listen to me, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ここは、法廷だ。いいかげんな 発言は、やめてもらいたいな。
English: This is a court of law, and I expect you to refrain from making thoughtless statements!

Japanese: ‥‥シロウトが！
English: Amateur!

Japanese: ‥‥それで？　刑事。 そのメモには‥‥？
English: Well, Detective? Tell us what was written on that memo you found.

Japanese: それを千尋さんが書いたという 証拠はあるんですか！
English: Do you have proof it was Mia who wrote that!?

Japanese: もちろんッス！
English: Of course I do, pal!

Japanese: （自信マンマン、といった 感じだ‥‥まずいな）
English: (Uh oh... he sounded pretty confident. This might not be good...)

Japanese: “化学分析”とは、具体的には どういう‥‥？
English: What kind of "tests" were these again?

Japanese: え！　具体的、ッスか？ ええと‥‥たしか‥‥
English: Huh? What kind? Umm... well...

Japanese: 血液の成分の、ええと、 ヘロ‥‥ヘログモビン‥‥いや、
English: I hear they take the, um, little bits in the blood, the... er... hemo... hemogl...

Japanese: モヘグロ‥‥ヘモグロモビロン あれ？　モヘログロヘドモドグロモ
English: Hermo... goblins... hobgob... Er...? Herma-goblin bobbin...

Japanese: ‥‥しょ、証言をキョヒするッス！
English: I-I refuse to testify on this matter, sir!

Japanese: じ、自分は‥‥ センモン家じゃないッス！
English: I'm no expert on blood tests!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、いいでしょう。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Yes, that was quite clear. You may continue with your testimony.

Japanese: 助かったッス‥‥
English: Th-thanks, pal. I mean Your Honor Sir.

Japanese: イトノコギリ刑事。
English: Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: は、はッ！
English: Y-yeah?

Japanese: 来月の給与査定を楽しみに しておくことだ。
English: I think you can expect a pleasant bonus in your next pay check.

Japanese: ‥‥とほほ。
English: Oh? Oh hoh hoh.

Japanese: （ちょっとキノドクだったかな）
English: (That was a mess...)

Japanese: と、とにかくッ！
English: Right! Where was I?

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。
English: Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: 被害者が犯人の名前を書き残す ケースは、かなり多いんですか？
English: Do you get a lot of cases where the victim actually writes the killer's name?

Japanese: 推理小説やドラマなんかでは よく見かけるッス！
English: Sure! It happens all the time in books and the movies!

Japanese: 何をバカなこと言ってるんですか。
English: This isn't a movie, Detective.

Japanese: ぐっ。
English: Oof!

Japanese: 現実には、どうなんですか？
English: Let's talk about reality, shall we?

Japanese: ‥‥あまり、聞いたことないッス。
English: Umm... I guess, I haven't heard of many cases... no.

Japanese: 被害者が、妹の名前を 書き残すなんて‥‥
English: Don't you find it a little odd that the victim would write down a name?

Japanese: 不自然だとは 思いませんでしたか？
English: Especially the name of her own sister?

Japanese: そ、そッスね。実は、少し‥‥
English: Ah, yeah, actually, you got a point, pal.

Japanese: そこまでだ。
English: Stop right there.

Japanese: 証人がどう感じたかは 問題ではない‥‥。
English: The witness's opinion on the matter is irrelevant!

Japanese: 問題は、被害者が被告人の名前を 書き残して、告発していることだ。
English: The facts are clear: the victim wrote down the name of the accused...

Japanese: 自分を殺害した犯人として！
English: The victim told us the name of her killer!

Japanese: せ、静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: （‥‥今のは、かなり 不利にひびいたな‥‥）
English: (That didn't go so well...)

Japanese: そ、そッス！ 御剣検事の言うとおりッス！
English: Th-that's right! What he said!

Japanese: 血は、どっちの手の指に ついていましたか？
English: On which hand was the bloody finger, Detective?

Japanese: 右手ッス！
English: The right hand!

Japanese: （うーん‥‥千尋さんも 右ききだ）
English: (Hmm... she WAS right handed...)

Japanese: はっはっはぁ！ 残念だったッスね！
English: Hah hah hah! Nice try!

Japanese: （くそ‥‥読まれていたか‥‥）
English: (Uh oh... I guess it wasn't too hard to see what I was getting at there.)

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。
English: Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: １つ、たしかめておきたい ことがあります。
English: There's one thing I want you to clarify for me here.

Japanese: あなたは、被害者の千尋さんが この文字を書いて、
English: You say that the victim, Mia Fey, wrote this note.

Japanese: 被告人・綾里　真宵を 告発した‥‥と、
English: That she was accusing the defendant, Maya Fey?

Japanese: ホンキで、そう主張する つもりですか？
English: That's really what you're saying?

Japanese: な、なんスか？ そのいかがわしい言い方は！
English: Wh-what? This isn't one of those lawyer tricks, now, is it?

Japanese: アタリ前ッス！ 他に、書けた人間はいないッス！
English: Of course she wrote it! Who else could have!?

Japanese: ‥‥逆ですよ、刑事。
English: You have it backwards, Detective.

Japanese: 逆‥‥ッスと？
English: B-backwards?

Japanese: このメモ、被害者にだけは ゼッタイに書けたハズがない！
English: The victim is the only person who absolutely could NOT have written it!

Japanese: これは、あなた方、警察の資料だ。
English: This is a report from your department, Detective.

Japanese: “鈍器による一撃で即死”
English: "Immediate death due to a blow from a blunt object."

Japanese: ‥‥死んでるじゃないですかッ！
English: She died immediately!

Japanese: あれッ！
English: But...!

Japanese: “あれ”じゃないでしょう！
English: No "but"-ing your way out of this one, Detective!

Japanese: せ、静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: たしかに弁護人の 言うとおりです！
English: The defense has a point.

Japanese: 即死した人間に、 メッセージを残すことなど‥‥
English: Someone who died immediately wouldn't have the time to write anything down.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: シツレイだが、その解剖記録は、 いつ手に入れたものかな？
English: I beg your pardon, but when exactly did you obtain that autopsy report?

Japanese: い、いつ‥‥って‥‥。
English: Wh-when...!?

Japanese: た、たしか、事件が あった日だったかな‥‥。
English: I... I'm pretty sure it was the day of the murder.

Japanese: バカ言っちゃダメッス！
English: You're wrong there, pal!

Japanese: 解剖記録ができたのは、 事件の次の日ッス！
English: We didn't write an autopsy report 'til the day after!

Japanese: あ、そうでしたっけ。
English: Oh. Right.

Japanese: あれは、事件のあった 次の日のことでしたけど‥‥。
English: It was the day after the murder...

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、忘れちゃいました。
English: I'm... sorry, I forgot.

Japanese: ハッ！ 忘れた‥‥ねえ。
English: Hah! Forgot, you say?

Japanese: あれは、事件があった 次の日ッス。
English: It was the day after the murder, Your Honor Sir.

Japanese: わたしたのは自分ッス。
English: I was the one who handed it to him myself! Personally!

Japanese: あ、そうでしたっけ。
English: Oh. R-right.

Japanese: それがどうかしましたか？
English: The prosecution's point being...?

Japanese: その資料は、古いよ 弁護人。
English: That autopsy report is outdated, Your Honor.

Japanese: な、なんですって！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: 昨日、私の命令で再調査があった。
English: A second autopsy was performed yesterday, at my request!

Japanese: “被害者は、鈍器で１回殴られて、 ほぼ即死の状態だった”
English: "Death was almost immediate due to a blow from a blunt object..."

Japanese: “ただし、殴られてから数分間、 生きていた可能性を認める”
English: "But there is a possibility the victim lived for several minutes after the blow."

Japanese: ‥‥こういう結果が今朝、 私のところに届いているのだよ。
English: I received these results this morning.

Japanese: そ、そんな！
English: N-no way!

Japanese: つまり！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 被害者は、“マヨイ”と書くだけの 時間があったわけだ！
English: It's quite easy to imagine that the victim did have time to write "Maya"!

Japanese: 以上‥‥だ。
English: That is all.

Japanese: な、なるほど！
English: I see!
(Damn you, Edgeworth!)

Japanese: （カ、カッコイイこと しやがって‥‥！）
English: (I should have known you'd have something up your sleeve!)

Japanese: なんだ弁護人。‥‥その目は‥‥
English: Why, Mr. Wright, you look shocked!

Japanese: 何か、言いたいことでも？
English: Something you want to say?

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥。
English: Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: あなたは、被告人を有罪にするため ならば、手段をえらばないとか。
English: I've heard there's nothing you won't do to get your verdict...

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ、再調査など する必要があったんですか！
English: What reason could you possibly have had to request a second autopsy report?

Japanese: な、成歩堂くん！ ‥‥口をつつしみたまえ！
English: Mr. Wright! The defense will refrain from personal attacks on the prosecution!

Japanese: かまわない、裁判長‥‥。
English: No matter, Your Honor...

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: キミがなんと言おうと、 この記録は正式なものだ。
English: Say what you will, the evidence in this report is undeniable.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事！ あなたはインチキだ！
English: Detective Gumshoe! You're a sham!

Japanese: ぼくにこんな、ウソの記録を わたして‥‥
English: How could you give me a faulty report!?

Japanese: いや、じ、自分はその‥‥
English: Huh? I-I thought...

Japanese: イトノコ刑事‥‥。
English: Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: あなたを信じたぼくが バカだった、ということですか。
English: Are you calling me a fool because I believed your report?

Japanese: い、いや、あの自分は、 その‥‥。
English: Huh? Me? I-I'm not... huh?

Japanese: イトノコギリ刑事。
English: Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: ‥‥ひッ！
English: Urp!

Japanese: ‥‥ダメじゃないか。 間違った記録をわたすなんて。
English: I'm disappointed in you, handing him the wrong report like that.

Japanese: あ‥‥す‥‥ すまねッス‥‥。 Eh...? I...
English: I'm sorry, sir.

Japanese: キミのミスだ、刑事。
English: You are at fault, Detective.

Japanese: ‥‥来月の給与査定、 楽しみにしておきたまえ。
English: This isn't going to look good on your evaluation next month.

Japanese: ま、またッスか‥‥とほほ。
English: W-what? B-but... *sigh*

Japanese: 裁判長。 ‥‥この記録の、受理を。
English: Your Honor, I submit this report to the court.

Japanese: わ、わかりました。 受理します。
English: U-understood. The court accepts the evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 書きなおした。
English: Autopsy Report updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥いかがだろうか。
English: Well, Your Honor?

Japanese: 被害者が犯人を告発した、という 可能性は、きわめて高い。
English: The evidence strongly suggests the victim was identifying the killer.

Japanese: ‥‥そう考えるのが 自然でしょうな。
English: I suppose that's the obvious conclusion, yes.

Japanese: （くそう‥‥マズいな‥‥）
English: (Darn! This isn't good!)

Japanese: では、次の証人を 入廷させていただこう。
English: The prosecution would like to call its next witness.

Japanese: まさに殺人の瞬間を目撃した、 悲劇の少女をここへ！
English: This poor, innocent girl saw the murder with her own eyes!

Japanese: では、松竹　梅世さんを 入廷させてください！
English: Let the witness Miss April May take the stand.

Japanese: （‥‥あの女のどこが “悲劇”の“少女”だよ‥‥）
English: (Exactly what part of her is "innocent"...?)

Japanese: 証人の名前を。
English: Witness, your name, please.

Japanese: 松竹　梅世でぇす。 よろしくお願いしまぁす☆
English: April May! At your service! *wink*

Japanese: 証人が出ただけで、 そんなに盛り上がらない！
English: Order! An introduction should not require any reaction from the crowd!

Japanese: 証人も、ムヤミに ウインクしないように！
English: The witness will refrain from wonton winking!

Japanese: はぁい。
English: Aww... Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: （ヤッカイだな‥‥）
English: (This is not good...)

Japanese: （今ので、法廷中の男のハートを ガッチリ、ワシづかみだぞ‥‥）
English: (She's already captured the heart of every man in the court!)

Japanese: 事件のあった９月５日の夜、 あなたはどこにいたか？
English: Tell us, where were you on the night of September 5, when the murder occurred?

Japanese: えっとぉ、梅世はぁ、出張先の ビジネスホテル？　にいましたぁ。
English: Um... gee... I was, like, in my hotel room? Tee hee.

Japanese: ホテルにはぁ、その日の午後に チェックインしましたぁ。
English: I checked in right after lunch.

Japanese: 事件があった綾里法律事務所の、 すぐ向かい側にあるホテルか？
English: And this hotel is directly across from the Fey & Co. Law Offices?

Japanese: そうでぇす！
English: Mmm... that's right, big boy.

Japanese: ‥‥そこであなたが見たことを 《証言》してください。
English: Please testify to the court about what you saw.

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: あれは、夜の９時ごろでしたぁ。 梅世、窓を見たんです。
English: It was, like, 9:00 at night. I looked out the window, y'know!

Japanese: そうしたら！　長い髪の女の人が お、おそわれてるじゃないですか！
English: And then, oooh! I saw a woman with long hair being attacked!

Japanese: おそっていたのは、もちろん 被告席のちっちゃい子よぉ！
English: The one attacking her was the mousey girl sitting in the defendant's chair!

Japanese: 女の人は、攻撃をかわして 逃げたんですぅ。
English: Then the woman, like, dodged to one side and ran away!

Japanese: あの子は、それを追いかけて トドメを‥‥！
English: But that girl, she caught up to her and... and... She hit her!

Japanese: 女の人は‥‥ああ！
English: Then the woman with the long hair...

Japanese: 女の人はそれで ‥‥ぐったりと‥‥なっちゃって。
English: She kinda... slumped.

Japanese: ‥‥以上。梅世が見たのは、 そ・れ・だ・け。
English: The end. That's all I saw. Every little bitsy witsy! *wink*

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: いかがかな、裁判長。
English: Well, Your Honor?

Japanese: なるほど。ギモンの余地は ありませんな‥‥。
English: I see. It is a remarkably solid testimony.

Japanese: これ以上、時間をかけることも ありますまい‥‥。
English: I don't see a need to trouble the witness any...

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってください！
English: W-wait, Your Honor!

Japanese: なんですか？　弁護人。
English: Yes, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ぼくの《尋問》がまだです！
English: What about my cross- examination!?

Japanese: しかし、今の証言は、ほぼ カンペキだったと思いますが？
English: I thought the witness's testimony just now was quite... firm. Didn't you?

Japanese: ‥‥たしかキミは、 綾里　千尋の弟子だっけな‥‥。
English: Mr. Wright... I understand you were Ms. Mia Fey's understudy, were you not?

Japanese: 彼女のやり方ならば、 よく知っている。
English: You must know her techniques well.

Japanese: 人の証言のアラを探す、 ヒキョウなやり方‥‥。
English: Her cowardly way of finding tiny faults in perfectly good testimonies...

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥！
English: H-hey! How dare you!

Japanese: どうしますか？　弁護人。 ‥‥《尋問》をしますか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Will you cross-examine the witness?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、《尋問》を しても、ムダのようですね。
English: No... you're right. I guess there wouldn't be much point.

Japanese: クックックッ。 ‥‥わかってくれて、うれしいよ。
English: Heh heh heh. I'm glad you saw the error of your ways so soon!

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 以上が検察側の主張、だ。
English: The prosecution rests its case!

Japanese: （な、なんだって！ もう、おわり‥‥？）
English: (Wh-what? That's it!?)

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: い、いやいや。 ちょ、ちょっと待ってください！
English: W-wait! Hold on!

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか？ やっぱり《尋問》をする、と？
English: Yes, Mr. Wright? Changed your mind? Will you cross-examine the witness?

Japanese: しますします！ （このままじゃ、負けてしまう！）
English: Yes yes yes!
(I'd better, or I'll lose on the spot!)

Japanese: もちろん、させてもらいます。
English: I'll gladly proceed with the cross-examination.

Japanese: （御剣は、梅世に尋問されるのを 恐れている！）
English: (If only because I have a feeling Edgeworth doesn't want me to!)

Japanese: （あの証人‥‥。 どこかに、弱点があるはずだ！）
English: (She has to have some weakness!)

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥では、《尋問》を！
English: Very well, you may begin your cross-examination!

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: あれは、夜の９時ごろでしたぁ。 梅世、窓を見たんです。
English: It was, like, 9:00 at night. I looked out the window, y'know!

Japanese: そうしたら！　長い髪の女の人が お、おそわれてるじゃないですか！
English: And then, oooh! I saw a woman with long hair being attacked!

Japanese: おそっているのは、もちろん 被告席のちっちゃい子よぉ！
English: The one attacking her was the mousey girl sitting in the defendant's chair!

Japanese: 女の人は、攻撃をかわして 逃げたんですぅ。
English: Then the woman, like, dodged to one side and ran away!

Japanese: あの子は、それを追いかけて トドメを‥‥！
English: But that girl, she caught up to her and... and... She hit her!

Japanese: （証言はここまで、か）
English: (Hmm... that's it?)

Japanese: （たしかに、ムジュンらしい ムジュンは、見あたらない）
English: (Nothing really jumps out as a contradiction...)

Japanese: （しかし、どこかに スキがあるはずだ‥‥）
English: (There's got to be something in there somewhere!)

Japanese: （とにかく、 ゆさぶってみよう‥‥！）
English: (Maybe I'd better just press her on the facts a bit!)

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: Why did you do that?

Japanese: え、え？ 何が“どうして”なのぉ？
English: Huh? "Why"? Like, why what?

Japanese: どうして窓を見たんですか？
English: Why did you look out the window? Were you expecting to see something?

Japanese: え、そ、それは‥‥そのぉ。
English: Oh, well, um... gee!

Japanese: （なんだ？　なにげない 質問なのに、どうして‥‥？）
English: (What? That's it? She can't get out of this question that easily!)

Japanese: なんとなくぅ？　みたいな。
English: I sort of, y'know.

Japanese: そう！　フィーリングね！
English: I had a feeling!

Japanese: （どうも、答えをはぐらかそうと しているような気もするけど）
English: (Well, I have a feeling she's trying to avoid the question!)

Japanese: （‥‥もう少し、探りを 入れてみるか？）
English: (Maybe I should press a little harder on this one?)

Japanese: （‥‥深追いはキケンだ。 ここは、やめておこう‥‥）
English: (If I go too deep, I might not come back out alive... I'll back off for now.)

Japanese: （もう少し、 つっこんでみるか‥‥）
English: (Let's see how far I can run with this...)

Japanese: でも、何かキッカケがあったから、 窓を見たのでは‥‥？
English: Surely, you must have had a reason to look out your window at that time of night!

Japanese: う、うぐ‥‥！
English: I... oooh!

Japanese: 弁護人！　ヒレツな行為は やめてもらいたい！
English: Mr. Wright! I will not have you badgering my witness!

Japanese: ひ、“ヒレツ”‥‥？
English: B-badgering?

Japanese: ささいなことを聞くことで、 証人を動揺させる‥‥。
English: You insist on needling her with these trivial questions.

Japanese: そんなことが、ゆるされて よいものだろうか！
English: I really don't think it should be allowed.

Japanese: （‥‥そうだそうだァ！‥‥）
English: (Yeah! Yeah! Stop him!)

Japanese: （‥‥ヒキョーだぞォ！‥‥）
English: (The poor girl!)

Japanese: 静粛に。
English: Order!

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥以後、 気をつけてください。
English: Mr. Wright, you have been warned.

Japanese: （くそ‥‥ヒレツな男に されちまったぞ‥‥）
English: (Poor girl!? What about poor me!?)

Japanese: あなたは窓を見た。 ‥‥それから、どうなったか？
English: You looked out the window. What did you see next?

Japanese: 髪の長い女性‥‥ 綾里　千尋さんですか？
English: The woman with long hair... that was Mia Fey?

Japanese: そうよぉ。スラッとした、 まあまあ？　キレイなヒト。
English: Um-hmm! Slender, sort of, well, some people might say pretty, if that's your thing.

Japanese: （“まあまあ”ねえ‥‥）
English: (Your... thing?)

Japanese: ‥‥おそっていた人物は、目撃を？
English: And the person attacking her?

Japanese: どうしてそれが 被告人だとわかったんですか？
English: How do you know she was the defendant?

Japanese: え？　そ、それは‥‥
English: Huh? Well... y'know!

Japanese: た、体型が女の子だったしぃ。
English: S-she had a girlish physique. Women know these things.

Japanese: それに、 ちっちゃかったんだもぉん！
English: Look... I-I just know, okay?

Japanese: 現場には、少女の体型をして、 身長の低い人物は１人だった。
English: There was only one person at the scene of the crime with a short, girlish figure.

Japanese: ギモンの余地は、ない。
English: The testimony is bulletproof, Your Honor.

Japanese: （そうだなあ‥‥）
English: (He's right...)

Japanese: （‥‥たしかに、それは そのとおりだな‥‥）
English: (Her testimony certainly does make sense...)

Japanese: （法廷の人間は、今のところ あの証人の味方だ‥‥）
English: (And everyone in the court keeps siding with her.)

Japanese: （ヘタにイジらない方が いいだろう‥‥）
English: (I'd better not press too hard on this one.)

Japanese: ‥‥それで？ 被害者はどうなったか‥‥？
English: So then, tell us what happened to the victim.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！ 今の証言は、おかしいッ！
English: Hold on a minute! That testimony stinks!

Japanese: な、何がよぉ！
English: W-what!?

Japanese: 梅世さん‥‥あなたは、
English: Miss May, I'm willing to bet that...

Japanese: “攻撃”ですか‥‥？
English: She "dodged"? Dodged what!?

Japanese: ‥‥そう。凶器を持って なぐりかかったの！
English: Well... the attack!

Japanese: それで？　‥‥つづきを。
English: Please, continue your testimony.

Japanese: それが、なぜ被告人だと わかったんですか？
English: How did you know it was my client!?

Japanese: え？　だ、だってぇ‥‥
English: Huh? Well, I... gee!

Japanese: まずぅ‥‥ 体型が女の子だった！
English: First of all, she had a girl's physique!

Japanese: それにぃ、 ちっちゃかった！
English: And, and secondly, she was... she was small!

Japanese: そんなの、そこの子しか いないじゃなぁい？
English: Who else could it be but her!

Japanese: （そうだなあ‥‥）
English: (She has a point...)

Japanese: （‥‥たしかに、それは そのとおりだな‥‥）
English: (Her testimony certainly does make sense...)

Japanese: （法廷の人間は、今のところ あの証人の味方だ‥‥）
English: (And everyone in the court keeps siding with her.)

Japanese: （ヘタにイジらない方が いいな‥‥）
English: (I'd better not press too hard on this one.)

Japanese: （もう１回、証言を 聞いてみよう‥‥）
English: (Maybe I should just listen to the testimony again.)

Japanese: そもそも、本当に被告人を 目撃したんですか！
English: Did you really see the defendant at all!?

Japanese: 被告人を目撃したなんて、 ウソなんじゃないですか？
English: Are you telling the truth? Did you really see the defendant!?
Urp!

Japanese: 弁護人！ ど、どういうことですか！
English: Mr. Wright! What's the meaning of this?

Japanese: そうよ！　どういうことよぅ！ 梅世、信じらんなぁい！
English: Yes, what is the meaning! Somebody tell me because I'm clueless! About this, I mean!

Japanese: いいですか。
English: Okay...

Japanese: もし、本当に綾里　真宵を 目撃していたのなら‥‥
English: If you had really witnessed my client, Maya Fey...

Japanese: “体型”よりも先に、 “服装”が目につくはずなのです！
English: You would have noticed her clothes before noticing her physique!

Japanese: 彼女は、見てのとおり、 妙な和服を着ている！
English: No one wears clothes like this on a daily basis! Except her!

Japanese: ついでに、頭の上を、これまた 妙なカタチに結っている！
English: And I'm no expert on fashion, but her hairdo looks far from normal to me!

Japanese: しかし、証人の証言では、 そのことは完全にムシされている。
English: However, the witness's testimony mentions neither of these things!

Japanese: そんなことは あり得ないのです！
English: The testimony is bogus!

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥。
English: But... but!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥事件当夜も、その カッコウをしていたかどうか‥‥
English: Still, we don't know if she was dressed that way the night of the murder...

Japanese: そのカッコウだったのです！
English: She was, Your Honor!

Japanese: ぼくは見ました。そして‥‥
English: I saw her.

Japanese: さきほどの、イトノコ刑事も 見ているはずです！
English: And so did Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: どうですか！　梅世さん！
English: What do you say to that, Miss May?

Japanese: ‥‥くっ！ な、なぁにそれ？
English: Rowr! What are you trying to say, you mean lawyer!

Japanese: う、梅世、ちゃんと見てたわよぅ。
English: I-I saw what I saw.

Japanese: ただ、細かいこと話しても、 しかたないかな、って‥‥。
English: I... just didn't think all the trifling little details were necessary, darling.

Japanese: 証人。
English: Miss May.

Japanese: 見たことは、ちゃんと ゼンブ話すようにしてください。
English: The court would like to remind you to please omit nothing in your testimony.

Japanese: すみませぇん。 梅世、反省☆
English: I'm sorry, Your Honor. I'll be a good girl. I promise. *wink*

Japanese: ‥‥では、もう一度 《証言》を。
English: Your testimony again, if you would.

Japanese: （くそ‥‥そうカンタンには まいらないか‥‥）
English: (Damn, I almost had her!)

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: 梅世、ちゃんと細かいところまで 見てたんだからぁ！
English: I did see everything! I did!

Japanese: 被害者の女の人、最初の攻撃を かわして、右に向かって逃げたの。
English: The victim--the woman--dodged the first attack and ran off to the right.

Japanese: そしたら、和服の女の子が 追いかけていって‥‥
English: Then the girl in the hippie clothes ran after her...

Japanese: 持っていた凶器でなぐりつけたの！ 凶器だって、ちゃんと見たわよ！
English: And she hit her with that weapon! I saw it! I did!

Japanese: 時計よ時計。‥‥置時計ってやつ？ 《考える人》の形してたわ！
English: That... that clock! Um... the kinda statue-y clock? "The Thinker," I think?

Japanese: ‥‥どう？　こんなに ハッキリ、見てたんだから！
English: Well? Does the accuracy of my report not startle you? Tee hee!

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。
English: I... see.

Japanese: 最初からそこまで細かく 話してくれていればねえ‥‥。
English: I only wish you had been so detailed from the beginning.

Japanese: ‥‥では、《尋問》を おねがいします。
English: Please begin the cross-examination.

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: 梅世、ちゃんと細かいところまで 見てたんだからぁ！
English: I did see everything! I did!

Japanese: 被害者の女の人、最初の攻撃を かわして、右に向かって逃げたの。
English: The victim--the woman--dodged the first attack and ran off to the right.

Japanese: そしたら、和服の女の子が 追いかけていって‥‥
English: Then the girl in the hippie clothes ran after her...

Japanese: 持っていた凶器でなぐりつけたの！ 凶器だって、ちゃんと見たわよ！
English: And she hit her with that weapon! I saw it! I did!

Japanese: 時計よ時計。‥‥置時計ってやつ？ 《考える人》の形してたわ！
English: That... that clock! Um... the kinda statue-y clock? "The Thinker," I think?

Japanese: （さっきの梅世の証言は、 御剣に教え込まれたもの‥‥）
English: (Her previous testimony must have been what Edgeworth wanted her to say...)

Japanese: （そして、今回こそが、 梅世自身のコトバ‥‥か）
English: (So this was the testimony in her own words...?)

Japanese: （よし。コテンコテンにノシて、 ギュウと言わせてやろうかな）
English: (Time to press and squeeze the truth out of her! Figuratively, of course.)

Japanese: じゃあ、ぼくのことも 見ていましたか！
English: So, you saw me then, too?

Japanese: あったりまえじゃなぁい。
English: Of course!

Japanese: そのギザギザアタマ、 よっく覚えてるわぁ！
English: I'd remember that spiky hair anywhere!

Japanese: （ギザギザ‥‥）
English: (Spiky...?)

Japanese: 証人。弁護人を 攻撃しないように。
English: The witness will refrain from personal attacks on the defense attorney.

Japanese: いっけなぁい、 梅世、怒られちゃったぁ！
English: Aww, was I a bad girl? I'm sowwy.

Japanese: ‥‥ということですので、 つづけてください。
English: Very well... continue.

Japanese: ホテルにいたあなたから見て “右”ですか？
English: Is that "right" as in your right, as you looked from the hotel?

Japanese: ‥‥ええとぉ、おハシ持つ手が こっちだからぁ‥‥。
English: Um... which hand do I hold my knife in again...?

Japanese: そうね、右よ！ 間違いないでぇす。
English: Right! It was my right hand! Right?

Japanese: それで？ ‥‥つづきをおねがいする。
English: Satisfied, Mr. Wright? Please continue.

Japanese: 出口の方へ？
English: Toward the exit?

Japanese: 上面図を見てみたまえ。
English: Just look at the floor plan!

Japanese: ホテルから目撃できる エリアは、こんな感じだ。
English: The area that can be seen from the hotel is like... this.

Japanese: 犯人はここ、 被害者はここにいた。
English: The killer was here. The victim was here.

Japanese: 被害者が壁ぞいに、出口のドアへ 向かったら‥‥？
English: Now, if the victim were to move along the wall, toward the exit door...?

Japanese: なるほど‥‥右‥‥か。
English: Right... she would be going right.

Japanese: そうですね。
English: Indeed.

Japanese: ‥‥証人？ それから、どうなりましたか？
English: Will the witness please tell the court what happened next?

Japanese: 急に、とってつけたように “和服”と言いだしましたね。
English: How convenient for you to remember her "hippie" clothes!

Japanese: よけいなおせわですぅ！
English: That's what you--I mean, that's what she was wearing!

Japanese: ‥‥あ、そういえば、アタマを おだんごみたいに結んでいたわ！
English: Oh, and her hair was all done up like a bun!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
それで、どうなりましたか？
English: What happened then?

Japanese: その凶器は、どこから‥‥？
English: Where did this weapon come from?

Japanese: 現場にあったデスクの上から 取り上げたの！
English: She picked it up from the desk!

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。 その凶器、どんなものでした？
English: I see. What sort of weapon was it?

Japanese: トケイ‥‥ですか。
English: A... clock?

Japanese: （って、最近、これと同じような コトがあったような‥‥？）
English: (Didn't this come up in another testimony recently...?)

Japanese: ど、どうしたの？　‥‥なんか、 しんみりしたカオしちゃってぇ？
English: W-well? Don't look so sour, Mr. Lawyer. You can't win them all.

Japanese: （ついに、シッポをつかんだ！）
English: (No... but I have a feeling I'm on to something now!)

Japanese: 梅世さん。
English: Miss May.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは今、とんでもない コトを口走りましたね‥‥
English: What you said just now was quite... revealing.

Japanese: な‥‥なによぅ。 おどかすの、ナシにしてよね！
English: Revealing? Oooh, you'd like that, wouldn't you. Naughty Mr. Lawyer...

Japanese: この《考える人》を、 あなたは“置時計”だと言った。
English: You just said that this statue of "The Thinker" was a clock.

Japanese: しかし。見ただけで、 それがわかるはずがないんですよ。
English: But there's no way of knowing that just by looking at it!
Urp!

Japanese: 最近、同じような状況で、 これを“時計”と言った人がいた。
English: Another person in much the same position as you recently called this a "clock," too...

Japanese: その人物は‥‥ 殺人犯でしたよ！
English: And he was found guilty... of murder!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 梅世さん！　あなたはなぜ、これが “時計”だと知っていたのですか？
English: Miss May. Can you explain how you know this was a clock?

Japanese: うっ‥‥。
English: Oooh... urp!

Japanese: 証人は、犯行を目撃していた。 ‥‥大切なのは、その１点だけだ！
English: The witness saw the murder with her own eyes! That's all that's important here!

Japanese: 弁護人は、細かい点をつついて 目くらましをしようとしている！
English: The defense is trying to confuse the issue with trivial concerns!

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね‥‥。 その質問は、却下‥‥
English: Yes... yes, of course. You will withdraw your question, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: このやり方は、 ぼくの武器です！
English: But questions are all I have, Your Honor!

Japanese: これで、ぼくは 真犯人を見つけたことがあるッ！
English: And as you may recall, I've caught murderers with these questions before!

Japanese: （‥‥１回だけど！）
English: (Well, only once...)

Japanese: 成歩堂くんの異議を認めます。 証人は、質問に答えなさい。
English: Objection sustained. You may continue to question the witness.

Japanese: （うー、あぶないあぶない）
English: (Whew, that was close.)

Japanese: （この質問が却下されてたら、 勝負は決まっていたよ‥‥）
English: (If he stopped me there, the trial would be over!)

Japanese: え？　え？ ケッキョク、どういうことぉ？
English: Huh? What? So... what happens now?

Japanese: なぜ、凶器が“時計”と 知っていたか、答えるんです！
English: What happens now is you answer my question! How did you know it was a clock?

Japanese: ‥‥え、だ、だって‥‥
English: What...! Th-that's...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥う、梅世、聞いたから。
English: ...Because... I heard it?

Japanese: そう！　梅世、あの時計が 鳴るのを聞いたの！
English: Yes! I heard it say the time!

Japanese: あなたは、綾里法律事務所に 行ったことがあるんですか！
English: So, you've been to the law offices of Fey & Co.!

Japanese: ババ、バカなコト言わないでよぅ。 行くワケ、ないでしょお！
English: N-n-no! Hey, I didn't say that! Why would I go there!

Japanese: ホ、ホテルの部屋から 聞いたんですぅ！
English: I heard from my hotel room. Hee hee!

Japanese: 現場の綾里法律事務所と 板東ホテルは、とても近い。
English: The law offices of Fey & Co., where the murder took place, are very close to the hotel.

Japanese: 聞こえても、おかしくはない！
English: She could easily have heard the clock!

Japanese: どうですか？　弁護人。 これで納得しましたか？
English: Hmm. Well, Mr. Wright? Are you satisfied?

Japanese: いいえ！　まだです！ （納得しちゃったら負ける！）
English: No, Your Honor!
(I can't give up now!)

Japanese: なぜなら‥‥、
English: I'm not satisfied because...

Japanese: あなたは、ホテルにいた！
English: You were at the hotel!

Japanese: 向かいのビルで鳴った 時計の音が、聞こえるはずがない！
English: There's no way you could have heard a clock go off in the building next door!

Japanese: 聞こえなかったという 証拠はあるのかな？
English: You have proof that she could not?
Uh...

Japanese: いいかね、新人弁護士。
English: Amateurs, amateurs. Listen to me, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 法廷では、証拠がすべてなのだよ。 ‥‥覚えておくといい。
English: In the courtroom, proof is everything. Without it, you have nothing. You ARE nothing.

Japanese: じゃあ、ホントに聞こえたか どうか、これから実験を‥‥
English: Then I would like to propose a test to see if she really could have heard...

Japanese: 検察側は、拒否する！
English: The prosecution denies your request!

Japanese: な、なぜ！　理由は！
English: Wh-what!? On what grounds?

Japanese: 事件に直接関係のない、 ささいなことだから、だ‥‥。
English: This is a trivial matter with no direct bearing on the case at hand!

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。 御剣くんの異議を認めます。
English: Indeed. Objection sustained.

Japanese: （くそう‥‥違う方向に 切り変えた方がいいな‥‥！）
English: (Damn! Time to switch directions... quick!)

Japanese: では、もういいですか？ 成歩堂くん？
English: Ready to proceed, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: いいですか！
English: Your Honor, members of the court...

Japanese: あの時計は、鳴るはずがない！ ‥‥なぜなら、
English: It is inconceivable that the clock in question rang!

Japanese: その時計には、今は 機械が入っていないのです！
English: That clock is missing its clockwork!

Japanese: な、なぜ、あなたに そんなことが‥‥？
English: H-how could you possibly...?

Japanese: とにかく、調べてください！ 今、すぐに！
English: Just have a look... As soon as you can!

Japanese: あっ！
English: Oh!

Japanese: どうですか、裁判長！
English: See anything interesting, Your Honor?

Japanese: べ、弁護人の言うとおり‥‥、
English: It is as the defense says!

Japanese: こ、こわれていると思うんですよ！ その時計は！
English: I-I think it's broken! That clock's busted!
You "think"?

Japanese: と、とにかく！　裁判長、 調べてみてください！
English: J-just look at it! Your Honor, please examine the clock!
Hmm...

Japanese: あっ！
English: Oh!

Japanese: どうですか、裁判長！
English: See anything interesting, Your Honor?

Japanese: これを“こわれている”と 言うべきかどうか‥‥。
English: Well, I'm not sure I would call this "broken," but I doubt it could ring...!

Japanese: その時計は、電池が切れている はずだからです！
English: The batteries on that clock must be dead!
"Must be," Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 裁判長！　とにかく 調べていただけますか！
English: Your Honor, if you would inspect the clock...!

Japanese: う、む。‥‥かまいませんが‥‥。
English: Hmm... very well.

Japanese: あっ！
English: Oh!

Japanese: どうですか、裁判長！ 電池は‥‥？
English: Well, Your Honor? Are they...?

Japanese: いやはや、 電池どころじゃないですぞ！
English: This clock has graver problems than dead batteries!

Japanese: この時計には機械が入っていない！
English: This clock is missing its clockwork!

Japanese: カラッポです！
English: It's quite empty!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: これはどういうことですか！
English: Would you care to explain to the court the meaning of this?

Japanese: ごらんになったとおりです。
English: It is as you can see.

Japanese: 時計に機械は入っていなかった。 だから、鳴るはずはなかった。
English: The "clock" was empty. It couldn't have rung!

Japanese: したがって、この証人は‥‥、
English: Therefore, this witness...

Japanese: 大ウソつきですッ！
English: is a big, fat liar!
F-fat!?
Well, Miss May?

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ。
English: Tsk tsk.

Japanese: なかなか楽しませてくれるねぇ‥‥ 弁護士クン。
English: Quite a show you've put on for us, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （こいつ‥‥時計がカラッポ だったこと、知ってたな‥‥！）
English: (He knew the clock was empty! Somehow... he knew!)

Japanese: どうやらキミは、 １つ忘れているようだ。
English: I'm afraid you've forgotten one thing, however.

Japanese: たしかに、時計はカラッポだな。 ‥‥鳴るはずがない。
English: Indeed, the clock is empty. As you say... it can't ring.

Japanese: しかし。その時計の機械がいつ、 抜きとられたのか？
English: However, we must ask: when was the clockwork removed?

Japanese: 証人が音を聞いた後に抜きとられた なら、なんのムジュンもない‥‥。
English: If it was after the witness heard the clock, then there is no contradiction!

Japanese: ‥‥た、たしかに、その可能性は ありますね。
English: Hmm! That's true. That is a possibility.

Japanese: 梅世さんが時計の音を聞いた後、 機械は抜きとられたのかも‥‥。
English: The clock might have been emptied after she heard it.

Japanese: そのとおりだ、裁判長！
English: And that is exactly what happened, Your Honor!

Japanese: いかがかな、弁護人。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: あの機械がいつ抜きとられたのか、 証明できますか？
English: Can you prove when the clockwork was removed?

Japanese: ハッ！ ‥‥そんなこと、できるわけが‥‥
English: Ho hoh! Impossible, of course...

Japanese: できますよ。
English: I have proof.

Japanese: な、何ッ！
English: W-what!?

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥覚えてるよ。
English: Wasn't it you who told me "proof is everything"?

Japanese: 法廷では、証拠がすべて、 だったっけ？
English: Well, I was listening.

Japanese: ‥‥見せてやるよ。 あんたの大好きな“証拠”を！
English: And now I'll show you the "proof" you like so much!

Japanese: 時計の機械が抜きとられたのが いつだったかを示す、証拠品は‥‥
English: The evidence that proves when the clockwork was removed is...

Japanese: さあ、どうですか！
English: What do you say to that?

Japanese: え？　え？　どういうことぉ？ 梅世、わっかんなぁい。
English: W-what? I don't rightly understand what that's supposed to mean!

Japanese: 私もわかりませんな。
English: I don't rightly understand either.

Japanese: ハッ！
English: Ho hoh!

Japanese: （‥‥シッパイ‥‥か！）
English: (Uh oh... I think I blew it.)

Japanese: 弁護人‥‥。 いいかげんな発言は困ります。
English: Mr. Wright, please refrain from flippant presentations of evidence in the future.

Japanese: す、すみません！
English: S-sorry, Your Honor!

Japanese: これを見てください！
English: Take a look at this!

Japanese: ほう。何やらカワイイ 携帯電話ですな。
English: Hmm. That's a very cute cell phone.

Japanese: うっわぁ。 弁護士さん、女の子みたぁい。
English: Ooh hoo! You have a girlie phone!

Japanese: い、いや。これはその、 ぼくのじゃないんですよ！
English: W-wait! Wait! This isn't my phone!

Japanese: いいですか！
English: Listen!

Japanese: これは、被告人の携帯電話です！ ‥‥そして、こいつには、
English: This is the defendant's cell phone, and it contains a recording...

Japanese: 事件当日の、被害者との会話が 録音されているんです！
English: A recording of a conversation she had with the victim on the day of the murder!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: ひ、被告人の携帯電話だと‥‥！ 聞いてないぞ、私は‥‥
English: The defendant's cell phone!? Th-this wasn't brought to my attention!

Japanese: イトノコ刑事が見落としたんじゃ ないかな？
English: Perhaps Detective Gumshoe overlooked it?

Japanese: ぶつぶつ‥‥（来月の給与査定、 覚えておくがいい‥‥）
English: *grumble* (The good detective better remember he's up for evaluation soon...)

Japanese: （おやおや、カワイソウに‥‥）
English: (My heart goes out to you, Edgeworth. Not.)

Japanese: とにかく。 ‥‥聞いてみてください。
English: Let's hear the conversation.

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: 《とにかく、その“考える人”？ あずかればいいんだ》
English: [So you just want me to hold on to "The Thinker" for you, then?]

Japanese: 《そうよ》
English: [If you could.]

Japanese: 《ああ、でも、今はこの時計、 しゃべらないけど》
English: [Ah... I should probably tell you, the clock isn't talking right now.]

Japanese: 《えー、なんで？ ‥‥つまんない》
English: [Huh? It's not working? That's lame!]

Japanese: 《さっき、 時計の機械を抜いちゃったから》
English: [I had to take the clockwork out, sorry.]

Japanese: 《９月５日・午前９時２７分》
English: [September 5, 9:27 AM]

Japanese: ピー
English: *beep*

Japanese: ‥‥つまり。
English: Your Honor,

Japanese: 事件のあった日の午前中、 まだ証人がホテルに来る前から、
English: I think this recording makes it clear that the clockwork was already gone...

Japanese: 時計の機械は、すでに なかったわけです！
English: and this was recorded in the morning, before the witness even arrived at her hotel!

Japanese: く‥‥んくくぅっ！
English: Muh... muh... muh!?

Japanese: さあ！　梅世さん！ 聞かせてもらいましょうか！
English: Well, Miss May? Would you care to explain this to the court?

Japanese: なぜ、凶器が“置時計” と知っていたかを、ね！
English: Just how did you know that weapon was a clock!?

Japanese: た、たしか‥‥。
English: W-well...!

Japanese: たしか、‥‥そ、そうですぅ！
English: Well, isn't it o-obvious?

Japanese: あの時計、前に見たことが あるんですぅ！
English: I saw that clock before!

Japanese: んーと、どこのお店 だったかなぁ？
English: Um... what store was that again? I-I go to so many!

Japanese: 梅世、すっかり 忘れてましたぁ！
English: Oops! I forgot! *wink*

Japanese: 前に見たことがあった‥‥。 それなら、筋はとおりますね。
English: So the witness had seen it before. That would make sense.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。 これで問題ありませんか？
English: Does the defense have any objections, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: そうですね‥‥。 まあ、見たことがあれば‥‥
English: Oh right... well, if she had seen it before, I guess...

Japanese: （‥‥って、待てよ！）
English: (Wait a second!)

Japanese: では、そろそろ‥‥
English: Then, the court would like--

Japanese: 待ったぁ！ ちょ、ちょっと待ってください！
English: Hold on! P-please wait, Your Honor!

Japanese: な、なんですか？ やはり、問題が‥‥？
English: Y-yes...? So you do have an objection?

Japanese: え、ええ、まあ。
English: Um, yes, well...

Japanese: 弁護人！　困りますね。
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: すみません‥‥し、しかし！
English: Sorry, Your Honor, it's just...

Japanese: “前に見たことがある”という 証人の発言は‥‥
English: The witness claims she had "seen it before."

Japanese: ある証拠品と、 思いっきりムジュンしています！
English: But this directly contradicts a piece of evidence already submitted to this court!

Japanese: ほ、ほお‥‥。 では、提示してもらいましょうか。
English: Well then, let's see it.

Japanese: “証人が、時計を見たことは なかった”ことを示す証拠品を。
English: Please produce this evidence that will prove the witness had not seen the clock before.

Japanese: これが、その証拠ですよ！
English: Here is undeniable proof!

Japanese: ハッ。
English: Hah!?

Japanese: カンベンしてくれたまえ‥‥。 理解不能だ。
English: Please, this is a court case, not some surrealist performance art piece!

Japanese: ううむ‥‥。 考えなおしますか、弁護人？
English: Hmm... Perhaps the defense would like to change its mind?

Japanese: カンタンなことです。
English: It's simple.

Japanese: あの時計は、店に 並んだことなど、ないんですよ！
English: This clock was never in any store, ever!

Japanese: え、ええっ！
English: W-whaaat!?

Japanese: あれは、ぼくの友だちが作った ものでね。
English: A friend of mine made that clock.

Japanese: この世に ２個しかないんですよ。
English: Only two exist in the world.

Japanese: しかもそのうちの１個は、 警察が保管しています。
English: And the one that isn't here is in police custody!

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥！
English: I-impossible! Everything is sold in stores!

Japanese: さ。‥‥今度は、どんな 言いわけをしましょうか‥‥？
English: Miss May, I think it's high time you went shopping for a better excuse...?
Mmpf...

Japanese: ‥‥いや。もう、言いワケ しようがないかな‥‥？
English: Oh? Excuses not on sale today?

Japanese: う、ううううううううん‥‥
English: Oooh? Oh ho ho. Mmmrrrrph!

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおおお おおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Ooooorrrrrrr ggggghhhhhhh... hhh!!!

Japanese: 何だっつーのよ！ このギザギザアタマがぁ！
English: What's it to you, porcupine-head!?

Japanese: あんな時計のことなんか、 どうだっていいんだよ！
English: That stupid clock doesn't matter, okay!?

Japanese: あのコムスメがやったんだ！ とっとと死刑にしちまいな！
English: She did it! And she should die for it! Die!

Japanese: ま、ま、ま、待ちなさい！
English: W-w-whoa! Let's not get ahead of ourselves.

Japanese: こ、ここは法廷です。 証人、冷静になるように。
English: T-this is a court of law, and the witness will remain calm!

Japanese: ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ え、あ‥‥。
English: Hrrrgh... hrrrh... Hrrr... heh. Oh?

Japanese: や、やだぁ、う、梅世ったら‥‥。 ハア‥‥ハア‥‥。
English: Oh! Oh? Oh hoh ho! S-silly me! *grunt*

Japanese: ちょっぴり、マジ切れ？
English: Did I, um, like... lose it?

Japanese: しちゃったみ・た・い‥‥☆
English: I guess I did. Tee hee! *wink*

Japanese: （こ‥‥こわいよお‥‥）
English: (S-scary...)

Japanese: 証人。私からお聞きしよう。
English: Miss May, let me ask.

Japanese: あなたは、どうして、凶器が 時計だと知っていたんですか？
English: Tell me, how did you know the weapon was a clock?

Japanese: ‥‥ふう。やれやれ。
English: Hmm... oh dear.

Japanese: 弁護人。あなたの意見は どうですか？
English: Does the defense have an opinion on this... behavior?

Japanese: （ここが、勝負どころだな！）
English: (Okay, this is it!)

Japanese: ‥‥はい。 では、ぼくの考えを言います。
English: Yes, Your Honor. Allow me to explain how I see the truth of the matter.

Japanese: 松竹　梅世は、なぜ凶器が時計だと 知っていたのか？　それは、
English: Miss April May, you knew the weapon was a clock because...

Japanese: （ここまでくれば、矢張のときと 同じパターンで攻撃すれば！）
English: (This is familiar territory. I'll just use the same approach as with Larry.)

Japanese: 梅世さんは、その時計を実際に 手に取ったんです！
English: Miss May held that very clock in her hands!

Japanese: そ、それは、 いつのことですか！
English: Mr. Wright! When was this!?

Japanese: もちろん、被害者を 殴ったときです！
English: When she used it to strike the victim! When else?

Japanese: せ、静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 弁護人！　どういうことですか！
English: Mr. Wright! What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: 梅世さん！　あなたが 千尋さんを殺害したんです！
English: April May, you killed Mia Fey, I say!

Japanese: 彼女を殴ったときのはずみで、 あの時計は鳴った！
English: And when you struck, the force of the impact made "The Thinker" ring!

Japanese: あなたは、その音を聞いたんです！
English: That's when you heard it!

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥
English: Tsk tsk.

Japanese: ケッサクだな、キミは ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: You truly are a work of art, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: な、なんだと！
English: W-what's that supposed to mean!?

Japanese: 時計の中がカラッポだと 証明したのは、キミ自身だろう！
English: It was you who just proved that "The Thinker" was empty!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。 （鳴るワケないなそりゃあ‥‥）
English: Oh... (Urk! Of course it wouldn't ring!)

Japanese: それに、この証人には、カンペキな アリバイがあるのだよ‥‥。
English: What's more, the witness has a rock-solid alibi.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。
English: Miss May?

Japanese: この弁護士さんに、証明して あげるといい。
English: Perhaps you could explain to the poor, misguided Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 事件があった時間、ちゃんとキミは ホテルにいたんだ、とね‥‥。
English: You were in the hotel at the time of the murder.

Japanese: （証明できるはずがない！ 彼女がやったんだ！）
English: (S-she can't prove it! She did it!)

Japanese: ‥‥いいわぁ。 してあげるわよぅ！
English: It would be MY pleasure!

Japanese: そ、そんなバカな！
English: N-no way!

Japanese: うわぁ、サムぅい。いまどき “そんなバカな！”だってぇ！
English: Yes way, Mr. Lawyer.

Japanese: いい？　事件があったの、 夜の９時ごろでしょ？
English: Tee hee? Didn't the murder take place at 9:00 at night?

Japanese: 梅世、そのときボーイさんから ルームサービスを受け取ったの。
English: Gee, that's the exact time I ordered some room service from the hotel bellboy!

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみに、ボーイも それを覚えていた。
English: Incidentally, the bellboy corroborates the witness's story.

Japanese: したがって、彼女は現場には 行っていないのだ！
English: Ergo, she was not at the crime scene! Rock solid!

Japanese: 弁護人！　ムジツの女性に 向かって、なんということを！
English: Mr. Wright! You've just made a serious accusation to a perfectly innocent woman!

Japanese: す、すみません‥‥。 （大失敗だ‥‥）
English: S-sorry, Your Honor. (That... didn't go so well.)

Japanese: （しかし、そうなると‥‥）
English: (But, if that's the case...)

Japanese: （彼女はどうやって、《考える人》 が“時計”だと知ったんだ？）
English: (Then how did she know "The Thinker" was a clock!?)
(Wait!)

Japanese: 裁判長！ わかりました！
English: Your Honor, I figured it out!

Japanese: 松竹　梅世が“時計”のことを 知る方法は、
English: There is one other way Miss April May could have known it was a clock!

Japanese: もう、１つしか残されていない！ その証拠もあります！
English: One way alone! And I have proof!

Japanese: ほおお‥‥。 そんな証拠があるのですか‥‥。
English: Well... proof, you say?

Japanese: では、その証拠を 提示してもらいましょう。
English: Then, the court will examine your proof, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 証人はなぜ、《考える人》が “時計”だと知っていたか？
English: How did the witness know "The Thinker" was a clock?

Japanese: この証拠が物語っています！
English: This evidence reveals the truth of the matter!

Japanese: ‥‥何を物語っているのですか？
English: What does it reveal, exactly?

Japanese: 弁護人のユメ物語、かな？
English: The mad delusions of the defense, perhaps?

Japanese: （ヒドい言われようだな‥‥ 失敗か‥‥）
English: (Uh oh, bad reaction. Maybe that wasn't the thing...)

Japanese: そのカオでは、 まだコリていないみたいですね。
English: I'm afraid it has revealed a certain over-eagerness...

Japanese: ではもう一度、聞きましょう。
English: Let me ask again:

Japanese: 証人は、あの時計を手に 取ったことはない！
English: The witness had never held the clock in her hand!

Japanese: しかし彼女は、それが時計である という情報を聞いていたんです！
English: However, she had heard that it was a clock!

Japanese: “聞いていた”‥‥？
English: She "heard"...?

Japanese: そうです。
English: That is correct, Your Honor.

Japanese: それ以外に、彼女が《考える人》を 時計だと知る方法はない！
English: There is no other way she could have known "The Thinker" was a clock!

Japanese: そして、ぼくはその証拠品を 提示することができます！
English: And I can show you the proof!

Japanese: ほう‥‥ それはおもしろい。
English: Well, this is interesting.

Japanese: では、見せてもらいましょう。
English: Let's see it, then.

Japanese: 凶器が時計であることを、証人が “聞いていた”という証拠を！
English: Show me evidence proving that the witness had "heard" the murder weapon was a clock.

Japanese: これが、その証拠です！
English: This is my evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人がどこを指さして いるのか、理解できませんな‥‥。
English: I'm afraid I'm not sure where you are going with all of this, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: い、いや‥‥。 “どこ”と言われましても‥‥。
English: Um... "where"? Well, it's not so much a place as a... er...

Japanese: ハッタリはそこまでか‥‥弁護人？
English: Come to the end of your bluff, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: い、いやいや！ 本番はこれからです！
English: W-wait! Wait! That was, uh, practice! Here's the real evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥証拠を提出するのに 練習をしないように。
English: Mr. Wright, this court does not condone the presentation of evidence for "practice."

Japanese: では、もう一度 見せてもらいましょう。
English: Let's see your evidence, for real this time, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 被告人の携帯電話です。
English: The defendant's cell phone.

Japanese: さっき見ましたな。
English: Yes, we've seen that already.

Japanese: もう一度、被告人と被害者の 会話を聞いてください。
English: Take another listen to the conversation between the defendant and victim.

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: 《‥‥あ、お姉ちゃん！ どうしたの？　めずらしいね》
English: [Mia! What's up? You haven't called in a while.]

Japanese: 《うん。‥‥実は、 あずかってほしいものがあって》
English: [Well, actually there's something I want you to hold on to for me.]

Japanese: 《またー？　今度は何？》
English: [Again? What is it this time?]

Japanese: 《置き時計よ》
English: [It's... a clock.]

Japanese: 《考える人の形をしてて、 なんと、しゃべるの！》
English: [It's made to look like that statue, "The Thinker." And it tells you the time!]

Japanese: たしかに、会話の中で《考える人》 のことを話していますが‥‥、
English: They do mention "The Thinker."

Japanese: どうして証人が その会話を知っているのですか？
English: But how would the witness know of this conversation?

Japanese: 彼女が、その会話を知っていた という証拠は‥‥？
English: Do you have proof that she knew of the conversation?

Japanese: （いや、これは違うだろう！）
English: (Wait... no, this can't be right!)

Japanese: （ここで間違うワケには いかない！）
English: (I can't afford any mistakes now!)

Japanese: 弁護人！ 早くしてください！
English: Mr. Wright! Your evidence, please!

Japanese: これを見てください。
English: Have a look at this.

Japanese: あ、ああっ！ そ、それはぁっ！
English: Ah! Oooh! Th-that!? Eh heh...

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくが、梅世さんの部屋から 見つけたものです。
English: I found this in Miss May's room.

Japanese: 弁護人！ そ、それはいったい‥‥？
English: Mr. Wright! Please explain to the court what this is!

Japanese: これを見てください。
English: Have a look at this.

Japanese: あ、ああっ！ そ、それはぁっ！
English: Ah! Oooh! Th-that!? Eh heh...

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくが、梅世さんの部屋から 見つけたものです。
English: I found this in Miss May's room.

Japanese: 弁護人！ そ、それはいったい‥‥？
English: Mr. Wright! Please explain to the court what this is!

Japanese: 松竹　梅世さん。
English: Miss April May?

Japanese: あなたは、被害者・綾里　千尋さん の電話を盗聴していましたね？
English: You were tapping the victim, Ms. Mia Fey's phone, were you not?

Japanese: う、うぐっ‥‥！
English: Oooh. Oooooh!

Japanese: 裁判長！ これはもう、お話にならない！
English: Your Honor, This is irrelevant!

Japanese: しかし‥‥、
English: I'm not entirely sure that it is. Objection overruled.

Japanese: 証人が盗聴器を 持っていたのは気になります。
English: It troubles me that our witness was in possession of a wiretap.

Japanese: 弁護人は、証人が電話を 盗聴していたと主張するつもりか！
English: This is outrageous! Does the defense truly claim that the witness was tapping her phone?

Japanese: そりゃそうでしょう！
English: Absolutely!

Japanese: それならば、まず証明しなければ ならないことがある！　すなわち、
English: Even if that was the case, (which it's not) you still have to prove one thing!

Japanese: “被害者が電話の会話中に、凶器を 《時計》と言ったことがあるか？”
English: Did the victim ever say that the weapon was a clock on the phone?

Japanese: ‥‥そんなことが、キミに 証明できるのかッ！
English: Can you prove that? I THINK NOT!

Japanese: ‥‥できますよ！ ごくカンタンに、ね！
English: Oh yeah? I THINK I CAN! It's simple!
WHAAAAT!?

Japanese: ‥‥証拠があるんですよ‥‥。
English: Here's my proof.

Japanese: 被害者が、電話で凶器のことを “時計”と表現したという証拠は、
English: The proof that the victim said, on the phone, that the weapon was a clock is...

Japanese: これだと思います！
English: This!

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん。
English: I think...

Japanese: それは違うと思いますよ。
English: That's not going to prove anything, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん。
English: I think...

Japanese: い、いえ！　今のは ウォーミングアップです！
English: W-wait! That was just a warm up!

Japanese: ‥‥ふぅ。 では、今度はおねがいしますよ。
English: Hmm! Okay, once again. And I expect real proof this time!

Japanese: 被告人の携帯電話です。
English: I present the defendant's cell phone.

Japanese: さっき見ましたな。
English: Yes, we've seen that.

Japanese: もう一度、被告人と被害者の 会話を聞いてください。
English: Listen once more to the conversation between the defendant and the victim.

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: 《‥‥あ、お姉ちゃん！ どうしたの？　めずらしいね》
English: [Mia! What's up? You haven't called in a while.]

Japanese: 《うん。‥‥実は、 あずかってほしいものがあって》
English: [Well, actually there's something I want you to hold on to for me.]

Japanese: 《またー？　今度は何？》
English: [Again? What's it this time?]

Japanese: 《置き時計よ》
English: [It's... a clock.]

Japanese: 《考える人の形をしてて、 なんと、しゃべるの！》
English: [It's made to look like that statue, "The Thinker." And it tells you the time!]

Japanese: ‥‥松竹　梅世さん。
English: Miss April May!

Japanese: あなたは盗聴器を使い、 会話を聞いていた。
English: You used a wiretap to listen to this conversation!

Japanese: だから、《考える人》が 時計だと知っていた。
English: That's how you knew "The Thinker" was a clock!

Japanese: 違いますか！
English: Am I wrong!?

Japanese: 裁判長！ これはもう、お話にならない！
English: Your Honor, this is ridiculous!

Japanese: お話にならないかどうか、 証人の顔を見ていただきたい！
English: Your Honor, look at the witness's face? Does she seem amused to you!?

Japanese: 弁護側は、答えを要求します！
English: The defense demands an answer.
La... laaa... lrrrrgf!

Japanese: ‥‥証人。答えなさい！
English: Witness, answer the question. Did you tap her phone?

Japanese: ‥‥証人！
English: Miss May!

Japanese: うるせえんだよ！　気やすく 話しかけるんじゃないよ！
English: Shut up, all of you! What gives you the right to talk to ME like that!

Japanese: このタコがぁ！
English: You... you LAWYER!

Japanese: み、み、みんなで、みんなして、 う、う、うめよを‥‥
English: I-it's no fair! All of you g-ganging up on me like that...

Japanese: うめよを、いぢめるのねっ！ そうなのねっ！
English: Oh, so I'm the bad girl, is that it? Is that it!?

Japanese: う、うええ、うえええええええん！
English: Uh... uh... uwaaaaaaah!

Japanese: （今、法廷中の人間が、コイツの 素顔を見た！）
English: (That did it! The court's seen the real Miss April May now!)

Japanese: （トドメを刺すなら、今だ！）
English: (Now to deal the final blow!)

Japanese: （トドメを刺すなら、今だ！）
English: (Now to deal the final blow!)

Japanese: 梅世さん！
English: Miss May!

Japanese: な、何よこのタコ！ 気やすく話しかけるんじゃないよ！
English: What is it you little shrimp! Talk to me in that tone of voice will you!?

Japanese: あなたがやったんですね？
English: You killed her, didn't you!

Japanese: 静粛に！ ‥‥静かにしてください！
English: Order! There will be order!

Japanese: な、なんでそうなるのよ！
English: What? How can you possibly say that!? Are you mad?

Japanese: 梅世は、盗聴してただけ‥‥
English: A-all I did was a little wiretap...

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！
English: Oops!

Japanese: 盗聴は認めるわけですね？
English: So you admit you tapped her phone!

Japanese: ‥‥く、くくくぅぅぅ‥‥！
English: Heh... heh... hrrah!

Japanese: で、でもねぇ！
English: B-but wait!

Japanese: 殺人なんてヒドいコト、 梅世はしてないんだからね！
English: I didn't do ANYTHING bad like murder! I'm a good girl!

Japanese: じゃあ！
English: Really?

Japanese: ‥‥それを証明できますか？ （できるワケ、ないだろう‥‥）
English: Can you prove it!?
(No way can she prove it!)

Japanese: カッコつけてんじゃないよ！
English: You think you're so smart, Mr. Lawyer...

Japanese: できるわよ！ 決まってんでしょ！
English: But I can prove it! And I will!

Japanese: そ、そんなバカな！
English: You can't be serious! No way!

Japanese: ハン！　サムいオトコね。 いまどき“そんなバカな！”って！
English: Way, I say! Way! Oh, and I assure you I'm serious, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: ‥‥えへん！
English: Hmph!

Japanese: いい？　事件があったの、 夜の９時ごろでしょ？
English: Okay? So, the killing happened around 9:00 at night?

Japanese: 梅世、そのときボーイさんから ルームサービスを受け取ったの。
English: Why, that's just when I was getting room service from that sweet bellboy...

Japanese: る、るうむさーびす？
English: R-room service!?

Japanese: 梅世、アイスコーヒーを たのんだの。
English: Ice coffee, I believe it was?

Japanese: 知ってる？　アイスコーヒー。
English: Ice coffee? You know? Like normal coffee, but COLD.

Japanese: 早く飲まないと、氷が溶けて ただのコーヒーになっちゃうの。
English: If you don't drink it quick, the ice melts and then you have... regular cold coffee.

Japanese: あ、あいすこぉひぃ？
English: I-ice coffee...!?

Japanese: ウソだと思うんなら、 ボーイさんに聞いてみたらぁ？
English: Think I'm making this up? Ask the bellboy! *wink*

Japanese: つまり彼女は、現場に 行っていないということだ。
English: Ergo, the witness was not on the scene at the time of the murder!

Japanese: すると、どういうことに‥‥？
English: So, where does that leave us...?

Japanese: まことにイカンながら‥‥、
English: It is my great displeasure to inform you...

Japanese: 証人はどうやら、被害者の 電話を盗聴していたらしい。
English: That the witness appears to have been tapping the victim's telephone.

Japanese: しかし！　それはこの殺人事件とは まったく関係ない、別の犯罪だ。
English: However! That is a separate crime, with no bearing on the current case whatsoever!

Japanese: 被告人・綾里　真宵の殺人を 目撃したことは事実なのだッ！
English: Her testimony stands! She saw the defendant, Maya Fey, commit murder!

Japanese: （くそ‥‥このままじゃ 逃げられる‥‥！）
English: (No! They're going to let her just walk away!)

Japanese: （梅世と殺人事件を結びつけられ ないかぎり、勝ち目はない‥‥）
English: (There's no way I can win this unless I tie Miss May to the murder somehow...)

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥どうしますか？
English: Well, does the defense have anything to say?

Japanese: え、ええと‥‥。 （すると、残された手は‥‥？）
English: Um, well... (Come on, think of something!)

Japanese: ルームサービスを運んだ、 ボーイを尋問させてください！
English: The defense would like to call the hotel bellboy as a witness!

Japanese: そこに何か、 トリックがあるはずです！
English: There's something suspicious there, and I'm going to get to the bottom of it!

Japanese: うわ。やっぱりサムいヒトぉ。 いまどき“トリック”って‥‥。
English: I think you've sunken quite low enough already.

Japanese: 私は、その尋問に反対する！
English: I object to calling the bellboy!

Japanese: な、なぜですか！ その理由は！
English: W-why? What's your reason?

Japanese: “盗聴は、殺人とは関係ない” というのが、私の考えだからだ。
English: Because I hold that the wiretapping had nothing to do with the killing!

Japanese: ただし。
English: However...

Japanese: キミが１つ、条件を飲むなら、 ボーイの尋問に同意してもよい。
English: If you agree to one condition, I'll consent to calling this witness.

Japanese: 条件‥‥？
English: Condition...?

Japanese: ボーイを尋問した結果、松竹　梅世 のアリバイがくずせなかった場合、
English: If Miss April May's alibi is not called into question after you examine the bellboy...

Japanese: 当然、松竹　梅世は 犯人ではないということになる。
English: Then you will recognize that Miss April May was not the killer, thus she is innocent!

Japanese: その場合は、きみは綾里　真宵の 《有罪》を認める。
English: Therefore you must accept the verdict of "guilty" for Ms. Maya Fey!

Japanese: ‥‥それが条件だ。
English: That is my condition.

Japanese: （なんだそりゃあ！）
（ボーイの証言から、 何かを引き出せなければ‥‥）
English: (I'd better find something suspicious in that bellboy's testimony...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんは、その場で《有罪》 というワケか‥‥）
English: (Otherwise Maya will be declared "guilty" on the spot!)

Japanese: （‥‥どうする？）
English: (What should I do...?)

Japanese: （えーい、とりあえず やってみるか！）
English: (Alright! I've got nothing to lose! Except for... well, everything!)

Japanese: わかりました。 条件を飲みましょう！
English: Understood. I accept your condition.

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: バカが‥‥。 ワナにかかったか。
English: Fool... You fell right into my trap!

Japanese: （‥‥なんだって！）
English: (Uh oh!)

Japanese: あ、あの、やっぱり‥‥
English: Uh... um, wait...

Japanese: （‥‥くそっ！　さすがに そんな条件は飲めない！）
English: (Grr! I can't accept those conditions!)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 ボーイの尋問はやめます。
English: Very well. The defense will refrain from calling the bellboy as a witness.

Japanese: では引きつづき、この証人に対する 尋問をつづけてください。
English: I see. You may continue your cross-examination, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: わかりました。 尋問をつづけます。
English: Right. On with the cross-examination.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。これ以上 何を聞くというのか？
English: What exactly do you have left to examine, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 松竹　梅世は 盗聴を認めている。
English: Miss April May has admitted to the wiretap, yes.

Japanese: だが、それは殺人とは 関係のないことだ。
English: But that bears no relevance to the case at hand: murder!

Japanese: 関係があることは 証明できないからな！
English: There's no way you can prove any connection!

Japanese: （そうなると‥‥ もう、材料がないぞ！）
English: (Uh oh! Think! This can't be the end... but I'm out of evidence!)

Japanese: では、この証人に対する尋問は、 終了ということになりますね。
English: Then I believe the cross- examination is over.

Japanese: 御剣検事。 次の証人はいますか？
English: Mr. Edgeworth, does the prosecution have any other witnesses to call?

Japanese: ‥‥いいや。 これが最後の証人、だ。
English: None, Your Honor. She's the last.

Japanese: （なんだって！ このままじゃ、真宵ちゃんは）
English: (What!? B-but that means...)

Japanese: （間違いなく《有罪》だ！）
English: (Maya's guilty...!?)

Japanese: ま、待ってください！
English: W-wait! Your Honor!

Japanese: なんですか？　弁護人。
English: Yes, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: やっぱり、ボーイの尋問を ‥‥やります！
English: The defense would like to call the bellboy after all!

Japanese: クックックッ。
English: Tsk tsk tsk...

Japanese: そう来ると思ったよ。
English: As I thought!

Japanese: しかし。 もしボーイを尋問した結果、
English: May I remind you, dear Mr. Wright. Should you question the bellboy...

Japanese: 松竹　梅世のアリバイを くずせなかった場合は‥‥
English: and Miss April May's alibi prove to be solid...

Japanese: 自動的に、綾里　真宵が 《有罪》ということになる。
English: then, by default, your client Ms. Maya Fey will be pronounced "guilty"!

Japanese: それで、かまわないな！
English: Are you prepared to accept my condition?
(Edgeworth...)

Japanese: （完全に追いつめられて しまったみたいだ‥‥）
English: (He's got me backed into a corner...)

Japanese: （しかし、他にもう道はない‥‥）
English: (But I don't see any other way to take this!)

Japanese: かまいません！
English: I accept!

Japanese: よろしい！
English: Very well!

Japanese: では、ホテルのボーイを 入廷させてください！
English: The court calls the hotel bellboy to the stand!

Japanese: なぜ、盗聴を‥‥？
English: Why did you tap her phone?

Japanese: 答えてください！
English: Answer the question!

Japanese: どうして答えなくちゃ いけないのぉ？
English: Do I have to?

Japanese: 殺人事件とは ムカンケイでしょお？
English: Isn't this a murder trial? Isn't tippity-tapping er... irrelevant?

Japanese: （くそ‥‥御剣から指示された とおりに答えているな‥‥！）
English: (Gah! She's saying exactly what Edgeworth wants her to say.)

Japanese: いいですか。
English: Miss May.

Japanese: あなたは殺人事件の被害者を 盗聴していたんですよ？
English: You were tapping the victim's phone!

Japanese: ものすごくアヤシイじゃ ないですか！
English: I hardly call that "irrelevant"!

Japanese: 弁護人の言い方は気になりますが、 気持ちはわかります。
English: While this court does not condone the defense's tone of speech, he has a point.

Japanese: どうなのですか？　証人。
English: Well, Miss May? Do you have an explanation for the court?

Japanese: あなたが殺人とは無関係だと、 証明はできませんか‥‥？
English: Can you prove you had nothing to do with this murder, even though you tapped her phone?

Japanese: （フン、できるわけないさ！）
English: (Hah! I'd like to see her pull THAT off!)

Japanese: 弁護士さん、なぁにそのカオ？
English: Mr. Lawyer, I saw that evil, evil grin!

Japanese: “フン、できるわけないさ！” とか思ってるんでしょ？
English: You were probably thinking "I'd like to see her pull THAT off," weren't you!

Japanese: （むむ！　やるな‥‥）
English: (Damn... she's good!)

Japanese: ザンネンでしたぁ！ ‥‥できるに決まってんでしょ！
English: Well you're not the first man who's thought that! And of course... I can, and will!

Japanese: 梅世さん‥‥。 あなたがやったんですね？
English: Miss May, confess. You did it, didn't you?

Japanese: な、バカなこと 言わないでよ！
English: Are you out of your mind!? Oh wait, I forgot... you're a lawyer! You must be!

Japanese: 事件が起こった時間、
English: At the time of the murder...

Japanese: 梅世はルームサービスを 受け取っていたんだから！
English: I was in my hotel room, getting room service!

Japanese: 梅世にできるワケ、 ないでしょ！
English: How could I have killed her?

Japanese: なんだったら、 ボーイさんに聞いてみてよ！
English: If you don't believe me, just ask the bellboy!

Japanese: 梅世さん‥‥。 なぜ、盗聴を？
English: Miss May... Why did you tap her phone?

Japanese: 答えてください！
English: Answer the question!

Japanese: どうして答えなくちゃ いけないのぉ？
English: Do I have to?

Japanese: 殺人事件とは ムカンケイでしょお？
English: Isn't this a murder trial? Isn't tippity-tapping er... irrelevant?

Japanese: （くそ‥‥御剣から指示された とおりに答えているな‥‥！）
English: (Gah! She's saying exactly what Edgeworth wants her to say.)

Japanese: いいですか。
English: Miss May.

Japanese: あなたは殺人事件の被害者を 盗聴していたんですよ？
English: You were tapping the victim's phone!

Japanese: ものすごくアヤシイじゃ ないですか！
English: I hardly call that "irrelevant"!

Japanese: 弁護人の言い方は気になりますが、 気持ちはわかります。
English: While this court does not condone the defense's tone of speech, he has a point.

Japanese: どうなのですか？　証人。
English: Well, Miss May? Do you have an explanation for the court?

Japanese: では、質問を始めさせていただく。
English: I believe we're ready for the witness to testify.

Japanese: ‥‥いかにも、ボーイのイメージに 忠実な男だ‥‥。
English: He certainly does look like a bellboy.

Japanese: ハイ。
English: Yes, sir.

Japanese: 仕事中にご招待をいただいて、 参上したしだいでございます。
English: I received your summons in the middle of work, sir. I'm happy to be of service.

Japanese: では、ティーセットも 重いでしょうから、
English: That tea set looks rather heavy, so without further ado,

Japanese: さっそく《証言》を おねがいします。
English: the witness may begin his testimony.

Japanese: かしこまりました。
English: Very good, sir!

Japanese: ～梅世のルームサービスについて～
English: -- Miss May's Room Service --

Japanese: ワタクシ、板東ホテルの ボーイ長をしております。
English: I am the head bellboy at the fine Gatewater Hotel, in business for four generations!

Japanese: あれは、夜の８時すぎでしたか。 梅世サマからお電話がありました。
English: I believe I received a call after 8:00 in the evening from our guest, Miss May.

Japanese: きっかり９時に、アイスコーヒーを 持ってきてほしいと。
English: She asked for an ice coffee to be brought to her at 9:00, on the dot, sir.

Japanese: それでワタクシ、きっかり９時に 持っていったしだいでございます。
English: I brought it to her at precisely the requested time, of course.

Japanese: アイスコーヒーは、間違いなく そのご婦人にお渡しいたしました。
English: And I delivered the ice coffee to our guest Miss May, herself.

Japanese: では、弁護人。《尋問》を‥‥。
English: I see. The defense may begin its cross-examination.

Japanese: は、はい。 わかりました！
English: R-right! I'm ready. (I hope...)

Japanese: （このボーイの尋問で、）
English: (This is it...)

Japanese: （梅世が殺人に関係している 可能性を証明できないと、）
English: (If I can't prove Miss May was involved with the murder now...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんは‥‥ オシマイだ！）
English: (Maya will be finished!)

Japanese: ～梅世のルームサービスについて～
English: -- Miss May's Room Service --

Japanese: ワタクシ、板東ホテルの ボーイ長をしております。
English: I am the head bellboy at the fine Gatewater Hotel, in business for four generations!

Japanese: あれは、夜の８時すぎでしたか。 梅世サマからお電話がありました。
English: I believe I received a call after 8:00 in the evening from our guest, Miss May.

Japanese: きっかり９時に、アイスコーヒーを 持ってきてほしいと。
English: She asked for an ice coffee to be brought to her at 9:00, on the dot, sir.

Japanese: それでワタクシ、きっかり９時に 持っていったしだいでございます。
English: I brought it to her at precisely the requested time, of course.

Japanese: アイスコーヒーは、間違いなく そのご婦人にお渡しいたしました。
English: And I delivered the ice coffee to our guest Miss May, herself.

Japanese: （‥‥このボーイが、ウソをつく 理由はないはずだ‥‥）
English: (This bellboy wouldn't have any reason to lie...)

Japanese: （しかし、この証言から 何か見つけないと‥‥！）
English: (But I have to find something to use in his testimony!)

Japanese: （もう一度だ。 テッテイ的にゆさぶってやる！）
English: (One more time. I'll press him until he spills the beans... or his tea!)

Japanese: あの、仕事は何を しているんですか？
English: What exactly is it you do at the hotel?

Japanese: なんでもいたしますよ。
English: Why, anything required of me, sir.

Japanese: フロントでチェックイン・ チェックアウトの管理から、
English: I check in guests, I check out guests.

Japanese: お部屋のおそうじに ベッドメイキング‥‥、
English: I clean rooms, I make beds.

Japanese: ついでにルームサービスも お運びいたします。
English: I even deliver room service, sir.

Japanese: 梅世サマのチェックインも モチロン、ワタクシが。
English: I checked Miss May in personally.

Japanese: いつも、そんな 気取ったしゃべり方なんですか？
English: Are you always so... so prim?

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: つまらないことは 聞かなくてよろしい。
English: You will refrain from asking frivolous questions...

Japanese: 梅世さん本人の声でしたか？
English: Are you sure it was Miss May on the phone?

Japanese: 間違いございません。
English: Absolutely, sir.

Japanese: ど、どうしてそう 言いきれるんですか！
English: H-how can you be so certain!?

Japanese: お客様のチェックインは、 ワタクシ自身が担当いたしました。
English: I checked Miss May in personally, sir.

Japanese: そのとき、お美しい顔を拝見して、 お声も拝聴しております。
English: Not only did I see her in all her stunning radiance, but I also heard her voice.

Japanese: そのときから目をつけて‥‥、 いや、その、えへん。
English: And then I saw THEM, and I...
Ahem. Er... ahem!

Japanese: とにかく、よく覚えておりました。
English: The point being, I remembered her quite well, sir.

Japanese: それで、どうなりましたか？
English: Yes, what then?

Japanese: “きっかり９時”‥‥ですか？
English: 9:00 "on the dot," you say?

Japanese: ハイ。何度も念を押されました。
English: Yes. I confirmed that detail several times.

Japanese: テレビを見ていたそうで、 番組が終わったら飲みたいと‥‥。
English: She was watching a program on the TV, and wished to drink after she finished, sir.

Japanese: （‥‥９時。犯行の時間か）
English: (9:00... the time of the murder!)

Japanese: “きっかり９時”に？
English: "Precisely" 9:00, then?

Japanese: きっかりしっかりはっきりくっきり ９時でございました。 9:00 PM.
English: Precisely, exactly, and most definitely, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥その、妙な自信は、 いったいどこから‥‥！
English: How can you be so sure!?

Japanese: 梅世サマから、それはもう 強く念を押されていましたから。
English: Miss May was quite insistent that it be brought then.

Japanese: “ボーイさぁん、おねがいよぉ。 梅世ぉ、９時に飲みたいのぉ”
English: "Oh, bellboy? Tee hee! I'd like, like, ice coffee at exactly 9:00!"

Japanese: ‥‥みたいな感じで。
English: Something like that, sir.

Japanese: それでワタクシ、時報とともに ノックしたしだいでございます。
English: Therefore, I knocked on her door at the crack of 9:00, sir.

Japanese: （なんか、作為を感じるな‥‥）
English: (Why would she be so particular about the time?)

Japanese: 松竹　梅世本人に 間違いありませんか？
English: You are sure it was Miss April May herself?

Japanese: モチのロンでございます。
English: Ab-SO-lutely, sir.

Japanese: “モチのロン”‥‥？
English: "Ab-SO-lutely"...?

Japanese: “おふこおす”“もちろん”を、 ワタクシ風にコジャレてみました。
English: Yes, sir. As in, "so very absolutely," sir. It's an endearing mannerism of mine.

Japanese: ‥‥その、妙な自信は、 いったいどこから‥‥！
English: How come you're so very certain!?

Japanese: あの、ルームサービスを お持ちした際にですね‥‥。
English: Well, when I brought the room service, sir...

Japanese: せせ、せ、せっぷんを、その。 い、いただいたものですから。
English: S-she... the guest, sir, favored me w-with a, um, an "embrasser," sir.

Japanese: せ、せっぷん？
English: "Embrasser"!? Is that French for "embrace"?

Japanese: ええ。キッスのことです。 ホッペのここに、チュッと。
English: It's French for "kiss," sir. But not a french kiss, sir! More of a peck on the cheek.

Japanese: な、な、な、 なんでそんなことを‥‥？
English: Wh-why would she have done that...?

Japanese: きっとワタクシに、その。 ホの字だったのではないかと。
English: I believe, perhaps, she was momentarily swayed by my prim demeanor, sir.

Japanese: ワタクシ、もう忘れられなくて。
English: It was a moment I shall never, ever forget, sir.

Japanese: （ゼッタイ、不自然だ‥‥）
English: (Sounds pretty fishy to me...)

Japanese: （梅世め、自分を印象づけようと 工作していたな‥‥）
English: (I think our Miss May was up to something and wanted the bellboy to remember her!)

Japanese: ‥‥ダメだ！ （何も見つからない！）
English: It's no good! (There's nothing there!)

Japanese: （もう、おしまいか‥‥？）
English: (Is... is that it!?)

Japanese: クックックッ。
English: Tsk tsk.

Japanese: やっとわかってもらえたかね。
English: Finally, you understand.

Japanese: このボーイには、ウソをつく理由が ないのだよ‥‥。
English: This bellboy has absolutely no reason to lie!

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Now...

Japanese: そろそろ、タイクツな尋問は 終わりにしてもらえるかな‥‥？
English: If you have any decency, you will end this rather tedious cross-examination here!

Japanese: そうですね。
English: Hmm. It was a bit tedious.

Japanese: ‥‥では、証人 ごくろうさまでした。
English: The witness may leave the stand.

Japanese: （いいのか、このままで！）
English: (I can't let this happen... can I?)

Japanese: （こ、ここまできて あきらめてしまったら‥‥）
English: (If I give up now, I lose everything...)

Japanese: （なんのために弁護士になったか わからないじゃないか‥‥）
English: (If I just give up the case, I'd be giving up the very reason I became a lawyer!)

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってください！
English: W-wait! Please wait!

Japanese: な、なんですか弁護人。
English: Yes? Does the defense have something to add?
